messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).

No, Confucius did not write the Mencius (孟子). The Mencius is attributed to Mencius — also known as Mengzi — a Confucian philosopher who lived approximately 372–289 BCE, roughly a century after Confucius (551–479 BCE). While Mencius was deeply influenced by Confucian thought and considered himself a defender of the Confucian tradition, he was a distinct thinker who developed his own philosophical positions. The attribution of the Mencius to Confucius is a specific misattribution that scholars explicitly reject.
